name: SnoopElementGeometryFilter
summary: (No description provided in vendor docs for NemAll_Python_IFW_Input.SnoopElementGeometryFilter.)

constructors.name: SnoopElementGeometryFilter(bFindBaseGeometry, bFindAreaGeometry, bPerpendicularOnElement, bFindNonPassiveOnly, bSplitAreaGeometries, bIdentifyEmbeddedElement, bFindCompleteFootprint, splitElement3D)
constructors.signature: SnoopElementGeometryFilter(bFindBaseGeometry, bFindAreaGeometry, bPerpendicularOnElement, bFindNonPassiveOnly, bSplitAreaGeometries, bIdentifyEmbeddedElement, bFindCompleteFootprint, splitElement3D)
constructors.summary: Constructor Passive geometry is virtual geometry which is using for element snooping. Typical passive geometry is boundary area around text. This area has no edges like a Hatching, but if cursor is inside this area, then element must be snooped. Passive geometry has no points, perpendicular points, edges, etc. .
constructors.remarks: Constructor Passive geometry is virtual geometry which is using for element snooping. Typical passive geometry is boundary area around text. This area has no edges like a Hatching, but if cursor is inside this area, then element must be snooped. Passive geometry has no points, perpendicular points, edges, etc. .

params.name: bFindBaseGeometry
params.doc: If true, find the base geometry element (e.g. line from polyline)

params.name: bFindAreaGeometry
params.doc: If true, the service adds the nearest area geometry if nothing else is found

params.name: bPerpendicularOnElement
params.doc: If true, the service adds perpendicular geometry

params.name: bFindNonPassiveOnly
params.doc: If true, then only non passive geometry will be searching

params.name: bSplitAreaGeometries
params.doc: If true, find the line geometry element for area geometries (e.g line from polygon)

params.name: bIdentifyEmbeddedElement
params.doc: If true, find elements from embedded documents

params.name: bFindCompleteFootprint
params.doc: If true, find the complete footprint of a wall instead of the footprint of the wall tier

params.name: splitElement3D
params.doc: Split the 3D element geometry ---> in case of bFindBaseGeometry the split is always done to edges !!!
